body:
**Scope:** the **scrub custodian** (§6.3 step 1): continuously `list_fragments` each D server, fetch and **verify each fragment's checksum against the chunk map**, and on a mismatch treat the fragment as lost and **enqueue the chunk for reconstruction** — catching **bit rot before the data is needed** (the mirror of the read path's read-time checksum verification, §6.2). A checksum-failing shard is **never** fed to the decoder. Emit **scrub coverage** and **scrub-detected corruption rate**.

**Definition of done**
- [ ] scrub walks each store and verifies fragment checksums against the chunk map
- [ ] an injected bit-flip is detected, the fragment excluded, and the chunk enqueued for repair
- [ ] scrub coverage and scrub-detected corruption rate are emitted
- [ ] read-path checksum failures feed the same reconstruction queue

**Needs:** M3.3 (and M3.2 for enumerate). **Refs:** proposal 0005 §"The four custodian loops" (scrub); architecture §6.3, §6.2, §8.3.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.5 — Scrub custodian: checksum verification → bit-rot detection → enqueue repair
